table.name: Manifold Pressure Sensor Scaling
MPS Voltage to Manifold Absolute Pressure

table.description: This is the scaling for the manifold pressure sensor. The multiplier is applied to manifold pressure sensor voltage and the offset is added to the result to determine manifold absolute pressure.

table.name: Manifold Pressure Sensor Limits (CEL)
table.description: When manifold pressure sensor voltage is greater than or equal to the first value or less than the second value, over a specific period of time, a CEL will be triggered. The time delay is determined by the 'Manifold Pressure Sensor CEL Delay' table.

table.name: Manifold Pressure Sensor CEL Delays
table.description: This is the period of time for which the manifold pressure sensor voltage must exceed the threshold as specified by the 'Manifold Pressure Sensor Limits (CEL)' table in order for a CEL to be triggered.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling A
table.description: This is the open loop fuel map. Because there is no feedback in open loop, the actual AFR may differ from the values presented in this table. In addition, the ECU applies a long-term correction (A/F Learning) to open loop fueling from patterns it recognizes in closed loop fueling. Other compensations and minimum enrichment factors exist as well. Because the underlying values of this table are enrichment offsets relative to stoichiometric, AFRs leaner than 14.7, as presented, are not valid.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling B
table.description: This is the open loop fuel map. Because there is no feedback in open loop, the actual AFR may differ from the values presented in this table. In addition, the ECU applies a long-term correction (A/F Learning) to open loop fueling from patterns it recognizes in closed loop fueling. Other compensations and minimum enrichment factors exist as well. Because the underlying values of this table are enrichment offsets relative to stoichiometric, AFRs leaner than 14.7, as presented, are not valid.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor #1 Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the front oxygen sensor.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor #2 Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the front oxygen sensor.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor Rich Limit
table.description: This is the rich limit for the front oxygen sensor. Regardless of the scaling of the front oxygen sensor, it will not read richer than this value.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor Compensation (Atm. Pressure)
table.description: This is the compensation of the front oxygen sensor at different atmospheric pressures. Calculate the compensation as follows: ((Front O2 AFR - 14.7) x Compensation Value) + 14.7. Regardless of compensation, AFR will still be limited on the rich side by the 'Front Oxygen Sensor Rich Limit' table and limited to an AFR of 29.4 on the lean side.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (Load)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (ECT)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on coolant temp. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (ECT) Disable
table.description: When coolant temp is greater than or equal to this value, the 'CL Fueling Target Compensation (ECT)' is no longer applied.

table.name: CL to OL Delay (Atm. Pressure)
table.description: This is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of these delay values will be used depending on atmospheric pressure. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' map.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When throttle position is equal to or rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). used to determine the pause in this transition to open loop. When throttle position drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle Hysteresis
OL to CL Transition
table.data: Hysteresis Below 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle' Map Value

table.description: When throttle position is equal to or less than this hysteresis subtracted from the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle' map value, the potential transition from open loop to closed loop begins (dependent on the primary open loop fuel map value and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Load' threshold).

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When the base pulse width, ((2707.09/Injector Flow Scaling) * Engine Load (g/rev))), rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When the base pulse width drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay BPW Hysteresis
OL to CL Transition
table.data: Hysteresis Below 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (BPW)' Map Value

table.description: When the base pulse width is equal to or less than this hysteresis subtracted from the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' map value, the potential transition from open loop to closed loop begins (dependent on the primary open loop fuel map value and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle' threshold)

table.name: CL Delay Minimum (ECT)
table.description: When coolant temp is the less than this value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When coolant temp is greater than or equal to this value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed A
table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed B
(Condition) - Result
table.data: (Above) - Clear CL Delay if Engine Speed Counter Threshold Exceeded

table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value, a counter value is incremented. If engine speed remains equal to greater than the second value, the counter will be continue to be incremented and if it exceeds the 'CL Delay Engine Speed B Counter Threshold' value, the primary closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value, the counter value is set to zero and other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Engine Speed B Counter Threshold
table.description: This is the delay in clearing the primary closed loop delay value if engine speed is greater than or equal to the value determined by the 'CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed B' table.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) A
(Condition) - Result
table.data: (Above) - Clear CL Delay if Throttle Counter Threshold Exceeded

table.description: When the accelerator pedal opening % is the same or greater than the second value, a counter value is incremented. If the accelerator pedal opening % remains equal to greater than the second value, the counter will be continue to be incremented and if it exceeds the 'Closed Loop Delay (Accelerator Pedal)' value, the primary closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When the accelerator pedal opening % drops below the first value, the counter value is set to zero and other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Throttle A Counter Threshold
table.description: This is the delay in clearing the primary closed loop delay value if accelerator pedal opening % is greater than or equal to the value determined by the 'Closed Loop Accelerator Pedal' table.

table.name: Injector Latency
table.description: Injector latency (dead-time)

table.name: Injector Flow Scaling
table.description: This is the fuel injector constant represented with an estimated flow rating (gas only). The underlying raw value does NOT represent the injector flow rate and there is no standard for measuring the flow rate of injectors. Therefore, it should NOT be thought of as a value that is going to exactly match published rates for your injectors but as a means to get you in the general ball park as a starting point to tune from.

table.name: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width A (ECT)
table.description: This is the injector pulse width based on coolant temp when cranking the motor. Compensation tables may impact the final pulse width. Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. TGV status may also be involved. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width B (ECT)
table.description: This is the injector pulse width based on coolant temp when cranking the motor. Compensation tables may impact the final pulse width. Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. TGV status may also be involved. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width C (ECT)
table.description: This is the injector pulse width based on coolant temp when cranking the motor. Compensation tables may impact the final pulse width. Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. TGV status may also be involved. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width D (ECT)
table.description: This is the injector pulse width based on coolant temp when cranking the motor. Compensation tables may impact the final pulse width. Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. TGV status may also be involved. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (RPM)

scaling.units: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width Compensation (%)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on engine speed and coolant temp.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (MAP)

scaling.units: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width Compensation (%)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the manifold absolute pressure.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (Accelerator)

scaling.units: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width Compensation (%)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the accelerator pedal.

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment A
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment B
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation
table.description: Tip-in Enrichment is not active until the calculated additional IPW, as determined by the 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' table and with compensations applied, exceeds this value. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for active tip-in enrichment. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (RPM)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on engine speed.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (MRP)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on manifold pressure.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation A (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation B (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT) Activation
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for the 'Tip-in Enrichment D (ECT)' table to be active. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment counter is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in the 'Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset' table is exceeded.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset
table.description: When the period between tip-in enrichment application exceeds the threshold in this table, the applied tip-in enrichment counter is cleared. The period between tip-in enrichment application is a counter that is cleared when tip-in enrichment is applied and incremented when the tip-in enrichment routine is executed. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in this table is exceeded. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is the value that is compared to the 'Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold' for disabling tip-in enrichment.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold exceeded the 'Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset' threshold.

table.name: Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset
table.description: When the period between tip-in enrichment application exceeds the threshold in this table, the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is cleared. The period between tip-in enrichment application is a counter that is cleared when tip-in enrichment is applied and incremented when the tip-in enrichment routine is executed. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold is exceeded in this table. The applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle is the value that is compared to the 'Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Throttle Cumulative Threshold' for disabling tip-in enrichment.

table.name: A/F Learning Max Limit (ECT)
table.description: These are the maximum limits for A/F Learning #1 and #2 referenced by coolant temperature. A/F Learning #1/#2 is the long-term correction applied to fueling based on feedback from the oxygen sensors during closed loop operation.

table.name: A/F Learning Min Limit (ECT)
table.description: These are the minimum limits for A/F Learning #1 and #2 referenced by coolant temperature. A/F Learning #1/#2 is the long-term correction applied to fueling based on feedback from the oxygen sensors during closed loop operation.

table.name: A/F Learning Airflow Ranges
A/F Learning #1 and #2 Store/Apply Ranges
table.data: Max Range A / Min Range B
Max Range B / Min Range C
Max Range C / Min Range D

table.description: These are the airflow ranges in which the different long-term fuel trims are calculated in closed loop and applied to the same airflow ranges for both closed loop and open loop.

table.name: MAF Limit (Maximum)
table.description: This is the maximum airflow that will be used by the ECU. Airflow will be capped at this limit regardless of the airflow values in the 'MAF Sensor Scaling' table.

table.name: MAF Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the mass airflow sensor.

table.name: MAF Compensation (IAT)
table.description: This is the compensation of airflow based on intake temp.

table.name: Engine Load Limit (Maximum)
table.description: This is the maximum allowable engine load. Engine load will be capped at this limit regardless of actual engine load.

table.name: Engine Load Compensation (MP)
table.description: This is the compensation of engine load based on RPM and manifold pressure.

table.name: Base Timing A
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing B
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing C
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing D
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing E
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing F
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Idle
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode. The 'Base Timing Idle Minimum' value will also be applied if the vehicle speed threshold is met.

table.name: Base Timing Idle (Below Speed Threshold)
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when vehicle speed is less than or equal to the 'Base Timing Idle Vehicle Speed Threshold'

table.name: Base Timing Idle (Above Speed Threshold)
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when vehicle speed is greater than the 'Base Timing Idle Vehicle Speed Threshold'

table.name: Base Timing Idle Vehicle Speed Threshold
table.description: This value determines the vehicle speed threshold involved in determining the switch between multiple 'Base Timing Idle' tables.

table.name: Knock Correction Advance Max A
table.description: This is the maximum amount of knock-based timing advance (knock correction advance) that can be added to base timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (KC advance max map value * IAM) + feedback knock correction + fine learning knock correction.

table.name: Knock Correction Advance Max B
table.description: This is the maximum amount of knock-based timing advance (knock correction advance) that can be added to base timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (KC advance max map value * IAM) + feedback knock correction + fine learning knock correction.

table.name: Timing Compensation (IAT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on intake temperature.

table.name: Timing Comp Min Load (IAT)
table.description: The minimum load necessary in order for the 'Timing Compensation (IAT)' table to be active.

table.name: Timing Compensation A (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Timing Compensation B (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Timing Compensation C (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Timing Compensation D (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Feedback Correction Range (RPM)
table.description: This is the engine speed range in which feedback corrections can be made by the ECU. Feedback correction is the immediate negative correction to timing advance due to knock as determined by the knock sensor.

table.name: Feedback Correction Minimum Load
table.description: This is the minimum engine load where feedback correction can be made by the ECU. Feedback correction is the immediate negative correction to advance due to knock as determined by the knock sensor.

table.name: Feedback Correction Retard Value

table.data: Potential Change in Current Feedback Correction Per Knock 'Event'

table.description: The step value for each negative adjustment to current feedback correction.

table.name: Feedback Correction Retard Limit
table.description: The limit for feedback correction.

table.name: Feedback Correction Negative Advance Value

table.data: Change in Negative Feedback Correction After Each 'No Knock' Delay

table.description: When feedback correction is negative and the knock signal is then clear, feedback correction does not immediately reset to zero. Instead, the negative correction is increased by the value in this table for each time period that passes with no knock as determined by the 'Feedback Correction Negative Advance Delay' table. Feedback correction will never be greater than zero.

table.name: Feedback Correction Negative Advance Delay

table.data: 'No Knock' Delay Period for Negative Feedback Correction Advance

table.description: When feedback correction is negative, this is the delay period over which if the knock signal is clear, the negative feedback correction will be incremented by the value in the 'Feedback Correction Negative Advance Value' table. This process will continue as long as the knock signal remains clear and the delay periods are satisfied until feedback correction is zero.

table.name: Fine Correction Range (RPM)
Potential Fine Correction Stored Value Adjustments

table.description: This is the engine speed range in which changes to the fine learning knock correction table in RAM can be potentially made by the ECU. Changes are based on knock or the relative lack of knock as determined by the knock sensor. In addition, other requirements must be met before changes can be made.

table.name: Fine Correction Range (Load)
Potential Fine Correction Stored Value Adjustments

table.description: This is the load range in which changes to the fine learning knock correction table in RAM can be potentially made by the ECU. Changes are based on knock or the relative lack of knock as determined by the knock sensor. In addition, other requirements must be met before changes can be made.

table.name: Fine Correction Rows (RPM)
table.description: These are the engine speed ranges that make up the fine learning correction table stored in RAM. These rpm ranges, as well as the load ranges specified by the 'Fine Correction Columns (Load)' table, determine how each fine correction value is stored as well as applied.

table.name: Fine Correction Columns (Load)
table.description: These are the engine load ranges that make up the fine learning correction table stored in RAM. These load ranges, as well as the rpm ranges specified by the 'Fine Correction Rows (RPM)' table, determine how each fine correction value is stored as well as applied.

table.name: Fine Correction Retard Value

scaling.units: Potential Change in Fine Correction Stored Value Per Knock 'Event' (degrees of correction)

table.description: The step value for each individual negative adjustment to the fine correction learning table in RAM.

table.name: Fine Correction Retard Limit

table.data: Fine Correction Stored Value Negative Limit

table.description: The limit for each negative fine correction learning stored value.

table.name: Fine Correction Advance Value

table.data: Potential Change in Fine Correction Stored Value After Each 'No Knock' Delay

table.description: The step value for each individual positive adjustment to the fine correction learning table.

table.name: Fine Correction Advance Limit

table.data: Fine Correction Stored Value Positive Limit

table.description: The limit for each positive fine correction learning stored value.

table.name: Fine Correction Advance Delay

table.data: 'No Knock' Delay Period for Positive Change to Fine Correction Stored Value

table.description: This is the required minimum period of time with no knock, as determined by the knock sensor, before a potential positive adjustment to the fine correction learning table can be made.

table.name: Rough Correction Range (RPM)
table.description: This is the engine speed range in which changes to the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) can potentially be made by the ecu. When this and other specific requirements are met, the IAM is decreased when knock is encountered, as determined by the knock sensor, or the IAM is increased with the lack of knock over a specific period of time as determined by the 'Rough Correction Learning Delay (Increasing)' table. The enable range in the 'Rough Correction Range (Load)' table must also be satisfied for potential rough correction learning.

table.name: Rough Correction Range (Load)
table.description: This is the engine load range in which changes to the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) can potentially be made by the ecu. When this and other specific requirements are met, the IAM is decreased when knock is encountered, as determined by the knock sensor, or the IAM is increased with the lack of knock over a specific period of time as determined by the 'Rough Correction Learning Delay (Increasing)' table. The enable range in the 'Rough Correction Range (RPM)' table must also be satisfied for potential rough correction learning.

table.name: Rough Correction Minimum KC Advance Map Value

scaling.units: Current 'Knock Correction Advance Max' Map Value (degrees)

table.description: This is the minimum knock correction advance max map value in order to begin re-evaluation of the IAM after entering rough correction mode. This is one of several requirements that must be met.

table.name: Rough Correction Learning Delay (Increasing)

scaling.units: Rough Correction (IAM) Positive Learning Delay (counter threshold)

table.description: This is the required minimum period of time with no knock, as determined by the knock sensor, before a potential positive adjustment to the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) can be made when the IAM is being re-evaluated.

table.name: Advance Multiplier (Initial)

table.data: Post-Reset or Active Rough Correction Initial Reset Value

table.description: This is the initial value for the ignition advance multiplier (IAM). The IAM is set to this value after an ECU reset and at the beginning of a rough correction learning session where the IAM would be re-evaluated.

table.name: Advance Multiplier Step Value

table.data: Initial IAM Step Value for Active Rough Correction

table.description: This is the initial change in the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) when re-evaluation of the IAM begins during a rough correction learning session. When this starts, the IAM is reset to the 'Advance Multiplier (Initial)' value and the step value is added to or subtracted from this value depending on knock. The step value is reduced by half when, during this session, the IAM changes from increasing to decreasing, or vice versa. When the step value is 0, or the IAM hits 0 or 1.0 for a period of time, the IAM re-evaluation ends. This how the ECU determines that the IAM has settled on the appropriate value.

table.name: Intake Cam Advance Angle A (AVCS)
table.description: This map selects the degree of intake cam advance for the variable valve timing system.

table.name: Intake Cam Advance Angle B (AVCS)
table.description: This map selects the degree of intake cam advance for the variable valve timing system.

table.name: Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal)
table.description: This table determines the driver requested torque based on the accelerator pedal angle and engine speed. This value is used to determine the target throttle plate angle as determined by the 'Target Throttle Plate Position (Requested Torque)' table.

table.name: Target Throttle Plate Position (Requested Torque)
table.description: The target from the 'Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal)' table and engine speed are used to determine the target throttle plate opening.

table.name: Rev Limit A
table.description: These are the engine speeds at which the rev limiter is engaged and disengaged. When engine speed is equal to or exceeds the 'On' value, fuel cut is active, after which, if engine speed drops below the 'Off' value, fueling is resumed.

table.name: Rev Limit B
table.description: These are the engine speeds at which the rev limiter is engaged and disengaged. When engine speed is equal to or exceeds the 'On' value, fuel cut is active, after which, if engine speed drops below the 'Off' value, fueling is resumed.

table.name: Fuel Temp Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the fuel temp sensor.

table.name: Intake Temp Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling of the intake temp sensor.

table.name: Coolant Temp Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling of the coolant temp sensor.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes A (ECT)
table.description: These are thresholds based on coolant temp which, along with the mode specified by the vehicle speed threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for coolant temp range from 0 to 2. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the coolant temperature is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes B (ECT)
table.description: These are thresholds based on coolant temp which, along with the mode specified by the vehicle speed threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for coolant temp range from 0 to 2. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the coolant temperature is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes C (ECT)
table.description: These are thresholds based on coolant temp which, along with the mode specified by the vehicle speed threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for coolant temp range from 0 to 2. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the coolant temperature is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes (Veh. Speed)
table.description: These are thresholds based on vehicle speed which, along with the mode specified by the coolant temp threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for vehicle speed range from 0 to 3. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the vehicle speed is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Idle Speed Target A
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target B
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target C
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target D
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target E
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target F
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Checksum Fix
table.description: The checksum issue will be fixed automatically when the rom is saved (regardless of check box).

table.name: Force Pass Readiness Monitors
table.description: Off-road and racing use only. Must NEVER be enabled on vehicles that will be driven on public roads.

table.name: (P0000) PASS CODE (NO DTC DETECTED)
table.description: PASS CODE (NO DTC DETECTED). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0000) PASS CODE (NO DTC DETECTED)
table.description: PASS CODE (NO DTC DETECTED). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0011) CAMSHAFT POS. - TIMING OVER-ADVANCED 1
table.description: EXHAUST AVCS SYSTEM 1 RANGE/PERFORMANCE. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0021) CAMSHAFT POS. - TIMING OVER-ADVANCED 2
table.description: CAMSHAFT POSITION - TIMING OVER-ADVANCED OR SYSTEM PERFORMANCE (BANK 2). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0031) FRONT O2 SENSOR LOW INPUT
table.description: FRONT OXYGEN SENSOR CIRCUIT LOW (BANK 1 SENSOR 1). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0032) FRONT O2 SENSOR HIGH INPUT
table.description: FRONT OXYGEN SENSOR CIRCUIT HIGH (BANK 1 SENSOR 1). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0037) REAR O2 SENSOR LOW INPUT
table.description: REAR OXYGEN SENSOR CIRCUIT LOW (BANK 1 SENSOR 2). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0038) REAR O2 SENSOR HIGH INPUT
table.description: REAR OXYGEN SENSOR CIRCUIT HIGH (BANK 1 SENSOR 2). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0051) HO2S CIRCUIT LOW B2 S1
table.description: HO2S HEATER CONTROL CIRCUIT LOW (BANK 2 SENSOR 1). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0052) HO2S CIRCUIT HIGH B2 S1
table.description: HO2S HEATER CONTROL CIRCUIT HIGH (BANK 2 SENSOR 1). To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0057) HO2S CIRCUIT LOW B2 S2
table.description: HO2S HEATER CONTROL CIRCUIT LOW BANK 2 SENSOR 2. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0058) HO2S CIRCUIT HIGH B2 S2
table.description: HO2S HEATER CONTROL CIRCUIT HIGH BANK 2 SENSOR 2. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0076) INTAKE VALVE CIRCUIT LOW (BANK 1)
table.description: OSV SOLENOID VALVE L CIRCUIT MALFUNCTION LOW. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0077) INTAKE VALVE CONTROL HIGH (BANK 1)
table.description: OSV SOLENOID VALVE L CIRCUIT MALFUNCTION HIGH. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0082) INTAKE VALVE CONTROL LOW (BANK 2)
table.description: OSV SOLENOID VALVE L CIRCUIT MALFUNCTION LOW. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P0083) INTAKE VALVE CONTROL HIGH (BANK 2)
table.description: OSV SOLENOID VALVE L CIRCUIT MALFUNCTION HIGH. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.description: MASS AIR FLOW CIRCUIT LOW INPUT. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: (P2135) TPS A/B VOLTAGE

table.name: (P2138) TPS D/E VOLTAGE
table.description: THROTTLE/PEDAL POSITION SENSOR/ SWITCH 'D'/'E' VOLTAGE RATIONALITY. To disable this DTC, make sure the box above is unchecked. Off-road use only.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)
CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle Hysteresis
CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)
CL to OL Transition with Delay BPW Hysteresis
CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed A
CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed B
CL Delay Engine Speed B Counter Threshold
CL Delay Throttle A Counter Threshold
Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width A (ECT)
Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width B (ECT)
Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width C (ECT)
Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width D (ECT)
Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold
Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold
Base Timing Idle Vehicle Speed Threshold
Rough Correction Minimum KC Advance Map Value
(P0151) O2 SENSOR CIRCUIT LOW B2 S1
(P0152) O2 SENSOR CIRCUIT HIGH B2 S1
(P0154) O2 SENSOR CIRCUIT OPEN B2 S1
(P0157) O2 SENSOR CIRCUIT LOW B2 S2
(P0158) O2 SENSOR CIRCUIT HIGH B2 S2
AVLS Switchover Load Threshold 1
table.description: AVLS switchover curve used when operating state 0xFFFFCD9C equals 2. RPM input is 0xFFFFB544 and the normal-path load signal compared is 0xFFFFB46C. From low cam, high cam is requested when load is at least curve + Hysteresis A (0x7D480). From high cam, low cam is requested when load drops below the raw curve. Stock: RPM 1600, 2000, 2400, 2800, 3200, 3600, 4000 -> load 100, 100, 30, 28, 25, 15, 5.

table.name: AVLS Switchover Load Threshold 2
table.description: AVLS switchover curve used when operating state 0xFFFFCD9C equals 3. From low cam, high cam is requested when load 0xFFFFB46C is at least curve + Hysteresis B (0x7D484). From high cam, low cam is requested when load drops below the raw curve. Stock: RPM 2000, 2050, 2400, 2800, 3200, 3600, 4000 -> load 100, 100, 90, 50, 30, 10, 0.

table.name: AVLS High Cam Engage RPM
table.description: Hard RPM override: at or above this engine speed the high-cam latch mask 0x04 is set at RAM 0xFFFFCD9E. Stock: 4000. Pairs with AVLS High Cam Release RPM (stock 3800) for hysteresis; keep engage above release.

table.name: AVLS High Cam Release RPM
table.description: Below this engine speed the hard high-cam RPM override is released (load-based curves take over again). Stock: 3800. Must stay below AVLS High Cam Engage RPM.

table.name: AVLS Switchover Load Hysteresis A
table.description: Engage hysteresis added to Threshold 1 when operating state 0xFFFFCD9C equals 2. High cam engages at curve + this value and releases below the raw curve. Stock: 10.0.

table.name: AVLS Switchover Load Hysteresis B
table.description: Engage hysteresis added to Threshold 2 when operating state 0xFFFFCD9C equals 3. High cam engages at curve + this value and releases below the raw curve. Stock: 10.0.

table.name: AVLS Actuation Minimum RPM
table.description: Minimum engine speed gate for OSV (oil switching valve) actuation, checked in avls_osv_actuation_gate @ 0x405CC. Below this the high-cam actuation is not commanded even if requested. Stock: 3000.
